rulings.Case Title: 1161 TETSUO COMMERCIAL PROPERTY LLC VS. JOHN G ERWIN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 29, 2009, line 14, DEFENDANT JOHN ERWIN, BAY COUNTIES ELECTRIC INC, and, SURETY COMPANY OF THE PACIFIC DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained in part, and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to 1st, 3rd and 4th causes of action. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 2nd, 5th, 6th, 8th, 9th, and 10th causes of action. A breach of contract should be addressed through a contract remedy rather than a tort remedy. Equitable indemnity is only available among tort feasors who are jointly and severally liable for a plaintiff's injury. The right to implied contractual indemnity is predicated on indemnitor's breach of contract. Plaintiff fails to allege how Erwin's assignment of rights could constitute a breach of the underlying contract with plaintiff. 8th, 9th, and 10th causes of action are subject to the litigation privlege. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the 7th cause of action. Plaintiff to allege facts regarding interference unrelated to lien and other litigation activities. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: EMMA COURT LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al VS. UNITED AMERICAN BANK, A CALIFORNIA BANKING et al
